micro_checklists.title: Define landlord rental plan
steps: Choose self-management or licensed professional management
Define target lease term and occupancy date
List included appliances, services, parking, storage and amenities
Set pet, smoking and property-use policies consistent with law
Define emergency, maintenance and communication channels

micro_checklists.title: Prepare a rental property
steps: Inspect structure, utilities, locks, alarms, plumbing, electrical and heating
Complete repairs and remove health or safety hazards
Clean the dwelling and service included appliances
Verify permits for conversions, bedrooms and accessory units
Photograph the completed condition before marketing

micro_checklists.title: Assemble landlord property records
steps: Confirm ownership and authorized signatory information
Review landlord insurance and liability coverage
Collect permits, warranties, manuals and service records
Create vendor and emergency-contact lists
Create secure folders for applications, leases, repairs and accounting

micro_checklists.title: Set rent and lawful charges
steps: Compare similar current rentals
Calculate mortgage, tax, insurance, maintenance, vacancy and management costs
Check applicable rent caps, emergency price restrictions and local rules
Separate rent, utilities and legally permitted charges
Document the pricing decision and review date

micro_checklists.title: Create a compliant rental listing
steps: Describe the actual unit, rent, deposit, utilities, availability and amenities accurately
Use neutral language based on property features rather than preferred people
Avoid protected-class preferences or discouraging statements
Publish consistent information across channels
Retain the listing and revision history

micro_checklists.title: Define written screening criteria
steps: Choose lawful income, credit, rental-history and occupancy criteria
Define treatment of applicants using lawful income sources or assistance
Create a reasonable-accommodation process
Define application order, completeness and tie handling
Have California-specific criteria reviewed before use

micro_checklists.title: Process rental applications
steps: Give every applicant the same written criteria and process
Timestamp complete applications
Collect only authorized information and fees
Protect identity and financial data
Track consideration, selection, refund and retention deadlines

micro_checklists.title: Verify applicant information
steps: Verify identity without using inconsistent standards
Verify income using permitted evidence
Contact prior landlords and references using standardized questions
Obtain screening reports only with lawful authorization and purpose
Record facts used in the decision without protected-class information

micro_checklists.title: Document rental decision
steps: Compare the completed application to written criteria
Record approval, conditional approval or denial reasons
Send required adverse-action information when a consumer report influenced the result
Refund unused or unconsidered fees when required
Retain records under a privacy-conscious retention policy

micro_checklists.title: Prepare residential lease
steps: Identify parties, premises, term, rent and due date
State deposits, utilities, maintenance, parking, pets and occupancy rules
Include entry, repair, notice and communication procedures
Attach required disclosures and property-specific addenda
Remove conflicting, unlawful or blank terms before signature

micro_checklists.title: Collect move-in funds
steps: Calculate first rent, prorated rent and lawful deposit
Provide an itemized statement before payment
Use a traceable payment method and verify cleared funds
Issue receipts and record deposit separately from income
Do not release possession until the agreed prerequisites are complete

micro_checklists.title: Complete landlord move-in
steps: Walk through the property with the tenant when practical
Complete a signed condition report with dated photographs
Test keys, locks, alarms, appliances and included systems
Confirm utility responsibility and service status
Provide contacts, payment instructions and emergency procedures

micro_checklists.title: Operate rental tenancy
steps: Track rent and issue accurate receipts or statements
Maintain a communication and notice log
Triage repairs by safety and habitability priority
Schedule preventive maintenance and lawful entries
Reconcile income, expenses, deposits and vendor records monthly

micro_checklists.title: Review renewal or termination
steps: Calendar lease and statutory notice deadlines
Review performance, maintenance and current legal restrictions
Calculate any proposed rent change under applicable limits
Prepare a lawful renewal, change notice or termination strategy
Obtain legal review for contested or high-risk situations

micro_checklists.title: Complete landlord move-out
steps: Acknowledge notice and provide required inspection information
Document condition before and after possession returns
Collect keys and confirm vacancy
Separate ordinary wear from tenant-caused damage
Complete repairs, final utility handling and deposit accounting on time

micro_checklists.title: Comply with federal fair housing
steps: Train everyone involved in advertising, showing and screening
Apply identical availability and screening information
Maintain accommodation and modification request procedures
Avoid steering and protected-class questions
Preserve objective records and escalate discrimination concerns

micro_checklists.title: Comply with tenant screening consumer-report rules
steps: Obtain permissible purpose and applicant authorization
Use a reputable consumer-report provider
Protect and securely dispose of report data
When a report affects the decision, send the required adverse-action notice
Give the reporting company details and dispute rights

micro_checklists.title: Provide lead-based paint disclosure
steps: Determine whether the pre-1978 rule applies
Give the EPA-approved lead hazard pamphlet
Disclose known lead hazards and available records
Include the required lead warning statement in the lease
Obtain and retain signatures before the tenant is obligated

micro_checklists.title: Review current California landlord law
steps: Open the current DRE landlord-tenant guide
Check laws effective after the guide publication date
Identify statewide and local rules applicable to the property
Record source URLs and review date
Escalate unresolved legal questions to qualified counsel

micro_checklists.title: Run California application screening
steps: Provide written criteria and screening process with the application
Consider complete applications in the required order
Charge only applicants actually considered and only lawful actual costs
Issue an itemized receipt
Return required fees within applicable deadlines and provide report copies when required

micro_checklists.title: Manage California security deposit
steps: Determine the current statutory limit and whether a small-landlord exception truly applies
Combine all deposit-like charges when testing the limit
State the amount and permitted purposes in the lease
Issue payment evidence and preserve the move-in condition baseline
Do not label any residential security deposit nonrefundable

micro_checklists.title: Create landlord condition record
steps: Photograph every room, fixture, appliance and exterior responsibility area
Use date-preserving original files
Complete a written checklist noting wear, damage and cleanliness
Invite tenant corrections within a defined period
Store signed records and originals through deposit disposition

micro_checklists.title: Manage California habitability and repairs
steps: Provide a reliable written repair channel and emergency contact
Log receipt, severity, access attempts and communications
Prioritize water, sewage, electrical, heating, security and other health risks
Use qualified vendors and required permits
Document completion and tenant confirmation without retaliation

micro_checklists.title: Perform lawful landlord entry
steps: Confirm a legally permitted purpose
Use the required notice method and timing unless a valid exception applies
Schedule during normal business hours when required
Enter only for the stated purpose and minimize disruption
Record notice, access and work completed

micro_checklists.title: Evaluate California rent and termination rules
steps: Determine property and tenancy coverage under statewide rules
Verify any exemption and required exemption notice
Calculate lawful rent changes over the applicable period
Identify at-fault or no-fault grounds and relocation obligations
Use current notice language and legal review

micro_checklists.title: Handle California lease violation
steps: Document facts and review the lease
Choose the legally correct notice and cure opportunity
Serve notice using a permitted method
Do not lock out, shut off utilities, remove property or harass
Use the court unlawful-detainer process if unresolved

micro_checklists.title: Conduct California initial move-out inspection
steps: After notice, give the tenant written information about the inspection right
Schedule within the permitted period if requested
Inspect and identify potentially deductible conditions
Give the tenant the required itemized statement
Allow the tenant an opportunity to address identified conditions before move-out

micro_checklists.title: Complete California deposit accounting
steps: Take required or prudent post-move-out photographs before repairs
Limit deductions to lawful purposes and reasonable amounts
Collect invoices, receipts, estimates and labor details
Send the itemized statement and remaining deposit within 21 days
Send supplemental documentation when estimates were initially necessary

micro_checklists.title: Use local landlord fair-housing resources
steps: Identify the county fair-housing provider
Request education before adopting uncertain policies
Document accommodation requests and interactive communications
Seek neutral assistance for disputes
Retain referrals and outcomes

micro_checklists.title: Verify county rental health requirements
steps: Identify whether city or county has jurisdiction
Review environmental-health issues relevant to water, sewage, pests or waste
Correct known conditions before occupancy
Keep inspection and remediation evidence
Provide tenants the proper reporting contact

micro_checklists.title: Prepare rental emergency information
steps: Check address-specific flood and emergency maps
Confirm insurance exclusions and landlord coverage
Document utility shutoffs and emergency contacts
Provide tenant evacuation and reporting information
Review after major hazard or service changes

micro_checklists.title: Verify Manteca rental use
steps: Confirm zoning and lawful dwelling status
Verify bedrooms, converted spaces and accessory units are permitted
Check occupancy and parking constraints
Resolve conflicting records before advertising
Retain City confirmations or permit records

micro_checklists.title: Prepare Manteca code compliance
steps: Inspect exterior, interior, utilities and property maintenance
Search available permit history for material alterations
Correct public-health or safety hazards
Coordinate inspections when required
Retain closure evidence for any notice or permit

micro_checklists.title: Configure Manteca rental utilities
steps: Identify account-holder rules for water, sewer and solid waste
Decide which services remain landlord-held
State allocation and reimbursement rules clearly in the lease
Complete City start or transfer forms before occupancy
Record meter and account status at handoff

micro_checklists.title: Provide Manteca service orientation
steps: Provide collection day and cart rules
Explain prohibited waste and bulky-item procedures
Share utility outage and City service contacts
Document tenant responsibility for carts and access
Retain the orientation acknowledgment

micro_checklists.title: Respond to Manteca code enforcement
steps: Read the cited code, correction and deadline
Photograph current conditions
Contact the assigned officer when clarification is needed
Complete permitted correction and provide evidence
Track inspection, penalties and final closure

micro_checklists.title: Route Manteca rental dispute
steps: Separate private lease disputes from code or safety complaints
Send code matters to the City channel
Use San Joaquin fair-housing resources for discrimination or accommodation issues
Use legal counsel or court self-help for notices and eviction
Document referrals and avoid retaliation

micro_checklists.title: Transition Manteca rental utilities
steps: Record final meter and possession date
Submit required move-out or account transfer request
Confirm solid-waste cart and account status
Reconcile final tenant-responsible charges under the lease
Retain the final City bill and turnover confirmation
